sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Packaging Corporation of America Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) reported Q1 2026 net income of $171 million ($1.91 per share), with adjusted earnings of $215 million ($2.40 per share) and net sales of $2.4 billion. The company achieved a 22% margin in its Packaging segment, ran at full capacity, and expects most containerboard price benefits to materialize by Q3. PCA guided Q2 adjusted EPS to $2.33, anticipating strong packaging demand and higher corrugated volumes.

feed.title: Why this money manager owns Caterpillar, TSMC and a Canadian dividend ETF
feed.summary: Ladan Shokrgozar, a senior portfolio manager at Harbourfront Wealth Management, discusses her investment strategy amid market volatility. She emphasizes diversification across asset classes, including global equities, fixed income, and alternatives like private debt and real estate. Shokrgozar highlights her firm's top picks: Caterpillar, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), and the iShares Core MSCI Canadian Quality Dividend Index ETF, while also noting rotations out of high-interest savings accounts and trimming some high-performing tech stocks.

feed.title: Gartner projects IT spending at $6.31 Trillion in 2026 on AI surge
feed.summary: Gartner forecasts global IT spending to reach $6.31 trillion in 2026, a 13.5% increase, driven significantly by the surge in AI infrastructure and software. Data center systems are expected to see the highest growth, jumping 55.8% to nearly $788 billion, reflecting the rapid scaling of AI workloads. Software spending, particularly generative AI model development, is also a major growth engine, while device spending sees more modest growth due to rising memory costs.

feed.title: Cessna Skyhawk Selected by Turkish Airlines Flight Academy f
feed.summary: Textron Aviation announced that Turkish Airlines Flight Academy has purchased 10 new Cessna Skyhawk aircraft to expand its training fleet. This addition will support the growing demand for airline pilots across Europe. The academy, which already operates 66 Skyhawks, chose the aircraft for its reliability, performance, and operating efficiency, further standardizing its training operations.

feed.title: VICI Properties to close $1.16B Golden Entertainment deal April 30
feed.summary: VICI Properties Inc. has announced that it expects to close its $1.16 billion acquisition of seven casino properties from Golden Entertainment Inc. around April 30, 2026, after receiving all necessary regulatory and shareholder approvals. This deal involves VICI acquiring the land, real property, and improvements, then entering into a triple-net master lease with a newly formed entity controlled by Golden Entertainment's current chairman and CEO, Blake L. Sartini. Golden Entertainment shareholders will receive approximately 24.3 million shares of newly issued VICI stock as part of the transaction.

feed.title: Musk Says Tesla Has Begun Production of Its Cybercab Robotaxi
feed.summary: Elon Musk announced on social media site X that Tesla has started manufacturing its Cybercab robotaxi. This launch comes as Tesla faces a slump in global sales and fulfills a long-standing promise for the electric-vehicle maker. The Cybercab is intended to be a part of Tesla's Robotaxi network, operating with a mix of autonomous vehicles and those with human safety monitors.

feed.title: Costco China Operator Faces Lawsuit From Beijing Namesake Firm
feed.summary: Costco Beijing International Business Development has sued Costco China Investment, the official operator of Costco stores in China, over alleged reputation infringement and unfair competition. Costco Beijing claims Costco China issued false statements on its official WeChat account, damaging its reputation, while Costco China maintains it is the legitimate operator and has no relation to Costco Beijing. The dispute highlights potential issues with brand naming and intellectual property in the Chinese market, as Costco Beijing prepares to begin operations claiming to be the China headquarters for a separate "Costco Wholesale International" registered in New York.

feed.title: CSX Reports $3.48 Billion in Q1 Revenue as Operating Margins Hit 36%
feed.summary: CSX reported strong first-quarter results with operating income of $1.25 billion and net earnings of $807 million. Revenue increased by 2% to $3.48 billion, and total volume rose by 3%. The company also achieved an operating margin of 36% through cost-cutting measures and revenue growth, including increases in intermodal volume and chemical traffic.
